title: William Shakespeare dies
subtitle: English playwright and poet
fact: William Shakespeare was an English playwright, poet and actor.

title: George Washington dies
subtitle: Founding Father and first U.S. president; soldier and slave owner
fact: George Washington was a Founding Father and the first president of the United States, serving from 1789 to 1797.

title: Henry VIII of England dies
fact: Henry VIII was King of England from 22 April 1509 until his death in 1547.

title: Christopher Columbus dies
subtitle: Italian navigator, explorer, and colonist
fact: Christopher Columbus was an Italian explorer and navigator from the Republic of Genoa who completed four Spanish-based voyages across the Atlantic Ocean sponsored by the Catholic Monarchs, opening the way for the widespread European exploration and colonization of the Americas.

title: Anne Boleyn dies
subtitle: Second wife of Henry VIII of England
fact: Anne Boleyn was Queen of England from 1533 to 1536, as the second wife of King Henry VIII.

title: Isaac Newton dies
subtitle: English mathematician and physicist
fact: Sir Isaac Newton was an English polymath who was a mathematician, physicist, astronomer, alchemist, theologian, author and inventor.

title: Catherine II of Russia dies
subtitle: The eighth and greatest Emperor of Russia
fact: Catherine II, commonly known as Catherine the Great, was the Empress of Russia from 1762 to 1796.

title: Leonardo da Vinci dies
subtitle: Italian Renaissance polymath
fact: Leonardo di ser Piero da Vinci was an Italian polymath of the High Renaissance who was active as a painter, draughtsman, engineer, scientist, theorist, sculptor, and architect.

title: James VI and I dies
fact: James VI and I was King of Scotland as James VI from 24 July 1567, and King of England and Ireland as James I from the union of the Scottish and English crowns on 24 March 1603, until his death in 1625.

title: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart dies
subtitle: Austrian composer of the Classical period
fact: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart was a Classical composer and musician.

title: Louis XIV of France dies
fact: Louis XIV was King of France from 14 May 1643 until his death in 1715.

title: Benjamin Franklin dies
subtitle: American polymath and statesman
fact: Benjamin Franklin was an American polymath: a writer, scientist, inventor, statesman, diplomat, printer, publisher and political philosopher.

title: Charles I of England dies
fact: Charles I was King of England, Scotland, and Ireland from 27 March 1625 until his execution in 1649.

title: Anne of Great Britain dies
fact: Anne was Queen of England, Scotland, and Ireland from 8 March 1702, and Queen of Great Britain and Ireland following the ratification of the Acts of Union 1707 merging the kingdoms of England and Scotland, until her death in 1714.

title: Charles II of England dies
fact: Charles II was King of Scotland from 1649 until 1651 and King of England, Scotland, and Ireland from the 1660 Restoration of the monarchy until his death in 1685.

title: Henry VII of England dies
fact: Henry VII, also known as Henry Tudor, was King of England and Lord of Ireland from his seizure of the crown on 22 August 1485 until his death in 1509.

title: Miyamoto Musashi dies
subtitle: Japanese swordsman, philosopher, strategist, writer, artist, and rōnin
fact: Miyamoto Musashi was a Japanese swordsman, strategist, artist, and writer who became renowned through stories of his unique double-bladed swordsmanship and undefeated record in his 62 duels.

title: Louis XVI of France dies
fact: Louis XVI was the last king of France before the fall of the monarchy during the French Revolution.

title: Martin Luther dies
subtitle: German priest and theologian
fact: Martin Luther was a German priest, theologian, author, hymnwriter, professor, and former Augustinian friar.

title: Michelangelo dies
subtitle: Italian artist and architect
fact: Michelangelo di Lodovico Buonarroti Simoni, known mononymously as Michelangelo, was an Italian sculptor, painter, architect, and poet of the High Renaissance.

title: George II of Great Britain dies
fact: George II was King of Great Britain and Ireland, Duke of Brunswick-Lüneburg (Hanover) and a prince-elector of the Holy Roman Empire from 11 June 1727 (O.S.) until his death in 1760.

title: Edward VI of England dies
fact: Edward VI was King of England and Ireland from 28 January 1547 until his death in 1553.

title: James II of England dies
fact: James II and VII was King of England and Ireland as James II and King of Scotland as James VII from February 1685 until he was deposed in the 1688 Glorious Revolution.

title: Niccolò Machiavelli dies
subtitle: Italian diplomat and political and military theorist
fact: Niccolò di Bernardo dei Machiavelli was a Florentine diplomat, author, philosopher, and historian who lived during the Italian Renaissance.

title: Johann Sebastian Bach dies
subtitle: German composer
fact: Johann Sebastian Bach was a German composer and musician of the late Baroque period.

title: Charles V dies
fact: Charles V was Holy Roman Emperor and Archduke of Austria from 1519 to 1556, King of Spain from 1516 to 1556, King of Sicily and Naples from 1516 to 1554, and also Lord of the Netherlands and titular Duke of Burgundy from 1506 to 1555.

title: Peter the Great dies
fact: Peter I was the Tsar of all Russia from 1682 and the first Emperor of Russia from 1721 until his death in 1725.

title: Louis XV of France dies
fact: Louis XV, known as Louis the Beloved, was King of France from 1 September 1715 until his death in 1774.

title: William III of England dies
fact: William III and II, also known as William of Orange, was the sovereign Prince of Orange from birth, Stadtholder of Holland, Zeeland, Utrecht, Guelders, and Overijssel in the Dutch Republic from 1672, and King of England, Ireland, and Scotland from 1689 until his death in 1702.

title: Ferdinand Magellan dies
subtitle: Portuguese explorer in the service of Spain
fact: Ferdinand Magellan was a Portuguese explorer best known for planning and leading the 1519–1522 Spanish expedition to the East Indies.

title: René Descartes dies
subtitle: French philosopher, mathematician, and scientist
fact: René Descartes was a French philosopher, scientist, logician, and mathematician, widely considered a seminal figure in the emergence of modern philosophy and science during Renaissance era.

title: James Cook dies
subtitle: British explorer, cartographer and naval officer
fact: Captain James Cook was a British Royal Navy officer, explorer, and cartographer who led three voyages of exploration to the Pacific and Southern Oceans between 1768 and 1779.

title: John Locke dies
subtitle: English philosopher and physician
fact: John Locke was an English philosopher and physician, widely regarded as one of the most influential of the Enlightenment thinkers and commonly known as the "father of liberalism".

title: Maria Theresa of Austria dies
subtitle: Ruler of the Habsburg Dominions
fact: Maria Theresa was the ruler of the Habsburg monarchy from 1740 until her death in 1780, and the only woman to hold the position in her own right.

title: Akbar dies
fact: Akbar, also known as Akbar the Great, was the third Mughal emperor, who reigned from 1556 to 1605.

title: Adam Smith dies
subtitle: Scottish moral philosopher and political economist
fact: Adam Smith was a Scottish economist and philosopher who was a pioneer in the field of political economy and key figure during the Scottish Enlightenment.

title: Maximilien Robespierre dies
subtitle: French revolutionary lawyer and politician
fact: Maximilien François Marie Isidore de Robespierre was a French lawyer and statesman, widely recognised as one of the most influential figures of the French Revolution.

title: Peter III of Russia dies
subtitle: The seventh Emperor of Russia
fact: Peter III Fyodorovich was Emperor of Russia from 5 January 1762 until 9 July of the same year, when he was overthrown by his wife, Catherine II.

title: Voltaire dies
subtitle: French writer, historian and philosopher
fact: François-Marie Arouet, known by his pen name Voltaire, was a French Enlightenment writer, philosopher (philosophe), satirist, and historian.

title: Nicolaus Copernicus dies
subtitle: Polish mathematician and astronomer
fact: Nicolaus Copernicus was a Renaissance polymath who formulated a model of the universe that placed the Sun rather than Earth at its center.

title: Babur dies
subtitle: Founder of the Mughal Empire
fact: Babur was the founder of the Mughal Empire in the Indian subcontinent.

title: Frederick II of Prussia dies
fact: Frederick II was the monarch of Prussia from 1740 until his death in 1786.

title: Aurangzeb dies
fact: Alamgir I, commonly known by the title Aurangzeb, was the sixth Mughal emperor, reigning from 1658 until his death in 1707.

title: Hernán Cortés dies
subtitle: Spanish conquistador
fact: Hernán Cortés, 1st Marquis of the Valley of Oaxaca was a Spanish conquistador, military commander, explorer, captain general, and writer who led an expedition that caused the fall of the Aztec Empire and brought large portions of what is now mainland Mexico under the rule of the King of Castile in the early 16th century.

title: Shah Jahan dies
fact: Shah Jahan I, also called Shah Jahan the Magnificent, was the fifth Mughal Emperor from 1628 until his deposition in 1658.

title: Thomas More dies
subtitle: English statesman, lawyer and philosopher
fact: Sir Thomas More, venerated in the Catholic Church as a martyr and saint, was an English lawyer, judge, social philosopher, author, statesman, theologian and noted Renaissance humanist.

title: Caravaggio dies
subtitle: Italian painter
fact: Michelangelo Merisi da Caravaggio, known mononymously as Caravaggio, was an Italian painter active in Rome for most of his artistic life.

title: Guy Fawkes dies
fact: Guy Fawkes, also known as Guido Fawkes while fighting for the Spanish, was a member of a group of provincial English Catholics involved in the failed Gunpowder Plot of 1605.

title: Jean-Jacques Rousseau dies
subtitle: Genevan philosopher, writer, and composer
fact: Jean-Jacques Rousseau was a Genevan philosopher, philosophe, writer, and composer.

title: Raphael dies
subtitle: Italian painter and architect
fact: Raffaello Sanzio da Urbino, now generally known in English as Raphael, was an Italian painter and architect of the High Renaissance.

title: Nader Shah dies
fact: Nader Shah Afshar was the founder of the Afsharid dynasty of Iran and one of the most powerful rulers in Iranian history, ruling as the emperor of Iran (Persia) from 1736 to 1747, when he was assassinated during a rebellion.

title: Leonhard Euler dies
subtitle: Swiss mathematician, physicist, and engineer
fact: Leonhard Euler was a Swiss polymath who was active as a mathematician, physicist, astronomer, logician, geographer, music theorist and engineer.

title: Rembrandt dies
subtitle: Dutch painter and printmaker
fact: Rembrandt Harmenszoon van Rijn was a Dutch Golden Age painter, printmaker, and draughtsman.

title: Mary Wollstonecraft dies
subtitle: English writer and intellectual
fact: Mary Wollstonecraft was an English writer and philosopher best known for her advocacy of women's rights.

title: Vasco da Gama dies
subtitle: Portuguese explorer
fact: Vasco da Gama was a Portuguese mariner, explorer and nobleman.

title: Henry IV of France dies
fact: Henry IV, also known by the epithets Good King Henry or Henry the Great, was King of Navarre from 1572 and King of France from 1589 to 1610.

title: Benedictus de Spinoza dies
subtitle: Dutch philosopher
fact: Baruch (de) Spinoza, also known under his Latinized pen name Benedictus de Spinoza, was a philosopher of Portuguese-Jewish origin, who was born and lived in the Dutch Republic.

title: Francis Bacon dies
subtitle: English philosopher and statesman
fact: Francis Bacon, 1st Viscount St Alban was an English philosopher and statesman who served as Attorney General and Lord Chancellor of England under King James I.

title: Mary II of England dies
fact: Mary II was Queen of England, Scotland, and Ireland with her husband, King William III and II, from 1689 until her death in 1694.

title: Antonio Vivaldi dies
subtitle: Italian composer and violinist
fact: Antonio Lucio Vivaldi was an Italian composer, virtuoso violinist and impresario of Baroque music.

title: Robert Hooke dies
subtitle: English natural philosopher, architect and polymath
fact: Robert Hooke was an English polymath who was active as a physicist, astronomer, geologist, meteorologist, and architect.

title: Américo Vespucio dies
subtitle: Florentine explorer, financier, navigator and cartographer
fact: Amerigo Vespucci was an Italian explorer and navigator from the Republic of Florence after whom America is named.

title: Francis Drake dies
subtitle: English sailor and privateer
fact: Sir Francis Drake was an English explorer and privateer best known for making the second circumnavigation of the world in a single expedition between 1577 and 1580.

title: Francisco Pizarro dies
subtitle: Spanish conquistador who conquered the Inca Empire
fact: Francisco Pizarro was a Spanish conquistador, best known for his expeditions that led to the Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire.

title: Hieronymus Bosch dies
subtitle: Dutch painter
fact: Hieronymus Bosch was a Dutch painter from Brabant.

title: Ismail I dies
subtitle: Founder and first shah of Safavid Iran
fact: Ismail I was the founder and first shah of Safavid Iran, ruling from 1501 until his death in 1524.

title: David Hume dies
subtitle: Scottish philosopher, economist, and historian
fact: David Hume was a Scottish philosopher, historian, economist and essayist who is known for his highly influential system of empiricism, philosophical scepticism and metaphysical naturalism.

title: Blaise Pascal dies
subtitle: French mathematician, physicist, inventor, writer, and Christian philosopher
fact: Blaise Pascal was a French mathematician, physicist, inventor, philosopher, and Catholic writer.

title: Jahangir I dies
fact: Nur ud-din Muhammad Salim, known by his royal name Jahangir, was the fourth emperor of the Mughal Empire, reigning from 1605 until his death in 1627.

title: Guru Nanak dies
subtitle: First Sikh Guru and founder of Sikhism
fact: Gurū Nānak, also known as Bābā Nānak, was an Indian spiritual teacher, mystic and poet, who is regarded as the founder of Sikhism and is the first of the ten Sikh Gurus.

title: Walter Raleigh dies
subtitle: English statesman, soldier and writer
fact: Sir Walter Raleigh was an English statesman, soldier, writer and explorer.

title: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz dies
subtitle: German mathematician and philosopher
fact: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz was a German polymath active as a mathematician, philosopher, scientist, and diplomat who is credited, alongside Isaac Newton, with the creation of calculus in addition to many other branches of mathematics, such as binary arithmetic and statistics.

title: Erasmus dies
subtitle: Dutch Renaissance humanist, Catholic priest, and theologian
fact: Desiderius Erasmus Roterodamus, commonly known in English as Erasmus of Rotterdam or simply Erasmus, was a Dutch humanist, Christian theologian, and pioneering philologist and educationalist.

title: Selim I dies
fact: Selim I, also known as Selim the Grim or Selim the Resolute, was the sultan of the Ottoman Empire from 1512 to 1520.

title: Thomas Hobbes dies
subtitle: English philosopher
fact: Thomas Hobbes was an English philosopher and political theorist, best known for his 1651 book Leviathan, in which he expounds an influential formulation of social contract theory.

title: Giordano Bruno dies
subtitle: Italian Dominican friar, philosopher and mathematician
fact: Giordano Bruno was an Italian philosopher, poet, alchemist, astronomer, cosmological theorist, and esotericist.

title: Catherine I of Russia dies
fact: Catherine I was Empress of Russia from 1725 to 1727.

title: Miguel de Cervantes dies
subtitle: Spanish novelist, poet, and playwright
fact: Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra was a Spanish writer widely regarded as the greatest writer in the Spanish language and one of the world's pre-eminent novelists.

title: Elizabeth I of Russia dies
subtitle: The sixth Emperor of Russia
fact: Elizabeth or Elizaveta Petrovna was Empress of Russia from 1741 until her death in 1762.

title: George Frideric Handel dies
subtitle: German-British Baroque composer
fact: George Frideric Handel was a German-British Baroque composer well-known for his operas, oratorios, anthems, concerti grossi, and organ concerti.

title: Albrecht Dürer dies
subtitle: German painter, printmaker, mathematician, and theorist
fact: Albrecht Dürer, sometimes spelled in English as Durer or Duerer, was a German painter, printmaker, and theorist of the German Renaissance.

title: Philip IV of Spain dies
subtitle: King of Spain and Portugal
fact: Philip IV, also called the Planet King, was King of Spain from 1621 to his death and King of Portugal from 1621 to 1640.

title: Johannes Vermeer dies
subtitle: Dutch painter
fact: Johannes Vermeer was a Dutch painter who specialized in domestic interior scenes of middle-class life.

title: Peter Paul Rubens dies
subtitle: Flemish painter
fact: Sir Peter Paul Rubens was a Flemish artist and diplomat.

title: Humayun dies
fact: Nasir al-Din Muhammad, commonly known by his regnal name Humayun, was the second Mughal emperor, who ruled over territory in what is now eastern Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Northern India, and Pakistan from 1530 to 1540 and again from 1555 to his death in 1556.

title: Johannes Kepler dies
subtitle: German mathematician and astronomer
fact: Johannes Kepler was a German polymath who was an astronomer, mathematician, astrologer, natural philosopher and music theorist.

title: Joseph II, Holy Roman Emperor dies
fact: Joseph II was Holy Roman Emperor from 18 August 1765 and sole ruler of the Habsburg monarchy from 29 November 1780 until his death.

title: Juan Ponce de León dies
subtitle: Spanish explorer and conquistador
fact: Juan Ponce de León was a Spanish explorer and conquistador known for leading the first official European expeditions to Puerto Rico in 1508 and Florida in 1513.

title: Jonathan Swift dies
subtitle: Anglo-Irish satirist and essayist
fact: Jonathan Swift was an Anglo-Irish writer, essayist, satirist, and Anglican cleric.

title: Bayezid II dies
subtitle: The eighth Sultan of the Ottoman Empire
fact: Bayezid II was the sultan of the Ottoman Empire from 1481 to 1512.

title: Francis Xavier dies
subtitle: Spanish Catholic saint and missionary
fact: Francis Xavier, venerated as Saint Francis Xavier, was a Navarrese cleric and missionary.

title: Ferdinand I, Holy Roman Emperor dies
fact: Ferdinand I was Holy Roman Emperor from 1556, King of Bohemia, Hungary, and Croatia from 1526, and Archduke of Austria from 1521 until his death in 1564.

title: John Smith dies
subtitle: English soldier, explorer, writer
fact: John Smith was an English soldier, explorer, admiral of New England, and author.

title: Sandro Botticelli dies
subtitle: Italian painter
fact: Alessandro di Mariano di Vanni Filipepi, better known as Sandro Botticelli or simply Botticelli, was an Italian painter of the Early Renaissance.

title: Titian dies
subtitle: Italian painter
fact: Tiziano Vecellio, Latinized as Titianus, hence known in English as Titian, was an Italian Renaissance painter.

title: Charles VI, Holy Roman Emperor dies
subtitle: 18Th century Habsburg Holy Roman Emperor
fact: Charles VI was Holy Roman Emperor and ruler of the Austrian Habsburg monarchy from 1711 until his death, succeeding his elder brother, Joseph I.

title: Carl XII of Sweden dies
subtitle: The fifth and last emperor of Sweden
fact: Charles XII, sometimes Carl XII or Carolus Rex, was King of Sweden from 1697 to 1718.

title: Daniel Defoe dies
subtitle: English trader, writer, and journalist
fact: Daniel Defoe was an English writer, journalist, merchant and spy.

title: Diego Velázquez dies
subtitle: Spanish painter
fact: Diego Rodríguez de Silva y Velázquez was a Spanish Baroque painter, the leading artist in the court of King Philip IV of Spain and Portugal, and of the Spanish Golden Age.

title: Pieter Brueghel the Elder dies
subtitle: Flemish painter
fact: Pieter Bruegel the Elder was among the most significant artists of Dutch and Flemish Renaissance painting, a painter and printmaker, known for his landscapes and peasant scenes ; he was a pioneer in presenting both types of subject as large paintings.

title: Montesquieu dies
subtitle: French philosopher
fact: Charles Louis de Secondat, baron de La Brède et de Montesquieu, generally referred to as simply Montesquieu, was a French judge, intellectual, historian, and political philosopher.

title: El Greco dies
subtitle: Greek artist, painter, sculptor and architect
fact: Doménikos Theotokópoulos, most widely known as El Greco, was a Greek painter, sculptor and architect of the Spanish Renaissance, regarded as one of the greatest artists of all time.

title: Michel de Montaigne dies
subtitle: French author, philosopher, and statesman
fact: Michel Eyquem, Seigneur de Montaigne, commonly known as just Michel de Montaigne, was one of the most significant philosophers of the French Renaissance.

title: Molière dies
subtitle: French playwright and actor
fact: Jean-Baptiste Poquelin, known by his stage name Molière, was a French playwright, actor, and poet, widely regarded as one of the great writers in the French language and world literature.

title: Hayreddin Barbarossa dies
subtitle: Turkish admiral
fact: Hayreddin Barbarossa, also known as Hayreddin Pasha, Hızır Hayrettin Pasha, and simply Hızır Reis, was an Ottoman corsair and later admiral of the Ottoman Navy.

title: John III Sobieski dies
subtitle: King of Poland and Grand Duke of Lithuania
fact: John III Sobieski was King of Poland and Grand Duke of Lithuania from 1674 until his death in 1696.

title: Christina of Sweden dies
fact: Christina, a member of the House of Vasa, was Queen of Sweden from 1632 until her abdication in 1654.

title: Christiaan Huygens dies
subtitle: Dutch mathematician, physicist and astronomer
fact: Christiaan Huygens, Lord of Zeelhem, was a Dutch mathematician, physicist, engineer, astronomer, and inventor who is regarded as a key figure in the Scientific Revolution.

title: Hernando de Soto dies
subtitle: Spanish explorer and conquistador
fact: Hernando de Soto was a Spanish explorer and conquistador, who was involved in expeditions in Nicaragua and the Yucatan Peninsula.

title: Abbas the Great dies
subtitle: Shah of Safavid Iran
fact: Abbas I, commonly known as Abbas the Great, was the fifth Safavid shah of Iran from 1588 to 1629.

title: Henry Hudson dies
subtitle: English explorer b1565
fact: Henry Hudson was an English sea explorer and navigator during the early 17th century, best known for his explorations of present-day Canada and parts of the Northeastern United States.

title: Abel Tasman dies
subtitle: Dutch seafarer, explorer and merchant
fact: Abel Janszoon Tasman was a Dutch seafarer and explorer, best known for his voyages of 1642 and 1644 in the service of the Dutch East India Company (VOC).

title: Ivan VI of Russia dies
subtitle: The fifth Emperor of Russia
fact: Ivan VI Antonovich, also known as Ioann Antonovich, was Emperor of Russia from October 1740 until he was overthrown by his cousin Elizabeth Petrovna in December 1741.

title: Emmanuel Swedenborg dies
subtitle: Swedish 18th century scientist and theologian
fact: Emanuel Swedenborg was a Swedish polymath; a scientist, engineer, astronomer, anatomist, Christian theologian, philosopher, and mystic.

title: Georges Danton dies
subtitle: French revolutionary
fact: Georges Jacques Danton was a French politician and leading figure of the French Revolution.

title: Jacques Cartier dies
subtitle: Breton maritime explorer of North America
fact: Jacques Cartier was a French maritime explorer from Brittany.

title: Eugene of Savoy dies
fact: Prince Eugene Francis of Savoy-Carignano, better known as Prince Eugene, was a distinguished feldmarschall in the Army of the Holy Roman Empire and of the Austrian Habsburg dynasty during the 17th and 18th centuries.

title: Denis Diderot dies
subtitle: French Enlightenment philosopher writer and encyclopædist
fact: Denis Diderot was a French philosopher, art critic, and writer, best known for serving as co-founder, chief editor, and contributor to the Encyclopédie along with Jean le Rond d'Alembert.

title: Guru Tegh Bahadur dies
subtitle: The ninth Guru of Sikhism
fact: Guru Tegh Bahadur was the ninth of ten gurus who founded the Sikh religion and was the leader of Sikhs from 1665 until his beheading in 1675.

title: Túpac Amaru II dies
subtitle: Leader of a large Andean uprising against the Spanish in Peru
fact: Túpac Amaru II, c.

title: Afonso de Albuquerque dies
subtitle: Portuguese general, admiral, and statesman
fact: Afonso de Albuquerque, 1st Duke of Goa, was a Portuguese general, admiral, statesman, and conquistador.

title: Samuel de Champlain dies
subtitle: 16/17Th-century French explorer of North America
fact: Samuel de Champlain was a French explorer, navigator, cartographer, soldier, geographer, diplomat, and chronicler who founded Quebec City and established New France as a permanent French colony in North America.

title: Ivan III of Moscow dies
fact: Ivan III Vasilyevich, also known as Ivan the Great, was Grand Prince of Moscow and all Russia from 1462 until his death in 1505.

title: Huldrych Zwingli dies
subtitle: Protestant Reformation leader in Switzerland, Swiss Reformed Church founder
fact: Huldrych Zwingli was a Swiss Christian theologian, musician, and leader of the Reformation in Switzerland.

title: Anthony van Dyck dies
subtitle: Flemish Baroque artist
fact: Sir Anthony van Dyck was a Flemish Baroque artist who became the leading court painter in England after success in the Spanish Netherlands and Italy.

title: Bartolomeu Dias dies
subtitle: Portuguese explorer
fact: Bartolomeu Dias was a Portuguese mariner and explorer.

title: Yemelyan Pugachev dies
subtitle: Leader of a Russian peasant uprising
fact: Yemelyan Ivanovich Pugachev was an ataman of the Yaik Cossacks and the leader of the Pugachev's Rebellion, a major popular uprising in the Russian Empire during the reign of Catherine the Great.

title: François Rabelais dies
subtitle: French writer and humanist
fact: François Rabelais was a French writer who has been called the first great French prose author.

title: Diego de Almagro dies
subtitle: Spanish conquistador
fact: Diego de Almagro, also known as El Adelantado and El Viejo, was a Spanish conquistador known for his exploits and killings in western South America.

title: Juan Sebastián Elcano dies
fact: Juan Sebastián Elcano was a Spanish navigator, ship-owner and explorer best known for having completed the first circumnavigation of the Earth in the ship Victoria on the Magellan expedition to the Spice Islands.

title: Luca Pacioli dies
subtitle: Italian father of accounting
fact: Luca Bartolomeo de Pacioli, O.F.M. was an Italian mathematician, Franciscan friar, collaborator with Leonardo da Vinci, and an early contributor to the field now known as accounting.

title: Pedro Álvares Cabral dies
subtitle: Portuguese military commander and explorer
fact: Pedro Álvares Cabral was a Portuguese nobleman, military commander, navigator and explorer regarded as the European discoverer of Brazil.

title: Vasco Núñez de Balboa dies
subtitle: Spanish explorer, governor, and conquistador
fact: Vasco Núñez de Balboa was a Spanish explorer, governor, conquistador, adelantado, and a pirate.

title: Louis Antoine de Saint-Just dies
subtitle: Philosopher, military and political leader
fact: Louis Antoine Léon de Saint-Just, sometimes nicknamed the Archangel of Terror, was a French revolutionary, political philosopher, member and president of the French National Convention, a Jacobin club leader, and a major figure of the French Revolution.

title: Daniel Gabriel Fahrenheit dies
subtitle: German-Polish physicist and engineer
fact: Daniel Gabriel Fahrenheit FRS was a physicist, inventor, and scientific instrument maker.

title: Claudio Monteverdi dies
subtitle: Italian composer, string player, choirmaster, and priest
fact: Claudio Giovanni Antonio Monteverdi was an Italian composer, choirmaster and string player.

title: Guru Arjan dies
subtitle: The fifth Guru of Sikhism
fact: Guru Arjan was the fifth of the ten total Sikh Gurus.

title: Gerardus Mercator dies
subtitle: Flemish geographer, cosmographer and cartographer
fact: Gerardus Mercator was a Flemish geographer, cosmographer and cartographer.

title: Giorgio Vasari dies
subtitle: Italian painter, architect, writer and historian
fact: Giorgio Vasari was an Italian Renaissance painter, architect, art historian, and biographer known for his work Lives of the Most Excellent Painters, Sculptors, and Architects, considered the ideological foundation of Western art-historical writing, and still much cited in modern biographies of the many Italian Renaissance artists he covers, including Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo, although he is since regarded as including many factual errors, especially when covering artists from before he was born.

title: George Vancouver dies
subtitle: English naval explorer
fact: Captain George Vancouver was a Royal Navy officer and explorer best known for leading the Vancouver Expedition, which explored and charted North America's northwestern Pacific Coast regions, including the coasts of what became the Canadian province of British Columbia and the U.S. states of Alaska, Washington, Oregon and California.

title: John Napier dies
subtitle: Scottish mathematician, physicist, and astronomer
fact: John Napier of Merchiston, nicknamed Marvellous Merchiston, was a Scottish landowner known as a mathematician, physicist and astronomer.

title: John Fisher dies
subtitle: English Roman Catholic cardinal and saint
fact: John Fisher was an English Catholic prelate who served as Bishop of Rochester from 1504 to 1535 and as chancellor of the University of Cambridge.

title: Jacopo Tintoretto dies
subtitle: Renaissance painter
fact: Jacopo Robusti, best known as Tintoretto, was an Italian Renaissance painter of the Venetian school.

title: Anders Celsius dies
subtitle: Swedish astronomer, physicist, and naturalist
fact: Anders Celsius was a Swedish astronomer, physicist and mathematician.

title: Vasili III of Moscow dies
fact: Vasili III Ivanovich was Grand Prince of Moscow and all Russia from 1505 until his death in 1533.

title: Sigismund II Augustus dies
subtitle: King of Poland and Grand Duke of Lithuania
fact: Sigismund II Augustus was King of Poland and Grand Duke of Lithuania, the son of Sigismund I the Old, whom Sigismund II succeeded in 1548.

title: Thomas Müntzer dies
fact: Thomas Müntzer was a German preacher and theologian of the early Reformation whose opposition to both Martin Luther and the Catholic Church led to his open defiance of late-feudal authority in central Germany.

title: Nicolas Poussin dies
subtitle: French painter
fact: Nicolas Poussin was a leading painter of the classical French Baroque style, although he spent most of his working life in Rome.

title: Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina dies
subtitle: Italian Renaissance composer
fact: Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina was an Italian composer of late Renaissance music.

title: Mary Wortley Montagu dies
subtitle: Noblewoman; writer and poet from England, editor
fact: Lady Mary Wortley Montagu was an English aristocrat, medical pioneer, writer, and poet.

title: Matteo Ricci dies
subtitle: Italian Catholic missionary
fact: Matteo Ricci was an Italian Jesuit priest and one of the founding figures of the Jesuit China missions.

title: Leo Africanus dies
subtitle: Andalusian author
fact: Johannes Leo Africanus was an Andalusi diplomat and author who is best known for his 1526 book Cosmographia et geographia de Affrica, later published by Giovanni Battista Ramusio as Descrittione dell'Africa in 1550, centered on the geography of the Maghreb and Nile Valley.

title: John Amos Comenius dies
subtitle: Czech teacher, educator, philosopher and writer
fact: John Amos Comenius was a Czech philosopher, pedagogue and theologian who is considered the father of modern education.

title: Giovanni da Verrazzano dies
subtitle: Florentine explorer of North America for France
fact: Giovanni da Verrazzano was an Italian explorer from the Republic of Florence, best known for his expedition to North America.

title: Camille Desmoulins dies
subtitle: French journalist, politician and revolutionary
fact: Lucie-Simplice-Camille-Benoît Desmoulins was a French journalist, politician and a prominent figure of the French Revolution.

title: Guru Ram Das dies
subtitle: The fourth Guru of Sikhism
fact: Guru Ram Das, sometimes spelled as Guru Ramdas, was the fourth of the ten Sikh gurus.

title: Frederick III of Denmark dies
fact: Frederick III was King of Denmark and Norway from 1648 until his death in 1670.

title: Moses Mendelssohn dies
subtitle: German-Jewish philosopher and theologian
fact: Moses Mendelssohn was a German-Jewish philosopher and theologian.

title: Philip Sidney dies
subtitle: English poet, courtier, diplomat
fact: Sir Philip Sidney was an English poet, courtier, scholar and soldier who is remembered as one of the most prominent figures of the Elizabethan age.

title: Guru Angad Dev dies
subtitle: The second Guru of Sikhism
fact: Guru Angad was the second of the ten Sikh gurus of Sikhism.

title: Gerolamo Cardano dies
subtitle: Italian Renaissance mathematician, physician, astrologer
fact: Gerolamo Cardano was an Italian polymath whose interests and proficiencies ranged through those of mathematician, physician, biologist, physicist, chemist, astrologer, astronomer, philosopher, music theorist, writer, and gambler.

title: Mikhail Lomonosov dies
subtitle: Russian polymath
fact: Mikhail Vasilyevich Lomonosov was a Russian polymath, scientist and writer, who made important contributions to literature, education, and science.

title: Luigi Galvani dies
subtitle: Italian physician, physicist, and philosopher
fact: Luigi Galvani was an Italian physician, physicist, biologist and philosopher who studied animal electricity.

title: Guru Amar Das dies
subtitle: The third Guru of Sikhism
fact: Guru Amar Das, sometimes spelled as Guru Amardas, was the third guru of Sikhism and became Sikh Guru on 26 March 1552 at age 73.

title: Antonio Pigafetta dies
subtitle: Venetian diplomat, historian, and explorer of the Pacific and Chile
fact: Antonio Pigafetta was a Venetian scholar and explorer.

title: William Dampier dies
subtitle: British pirate and explorer
fact: William Dampier was an English explorer, pirate, privateer and naturalist who became the first Englishman to explore parts of what is today Australia, and the first person to circumnavigate the world three times.

title: Johann Bernoulli dies
subtitle: Swiss mathematician
fact: Johann Bernoulli was a Swiss mathematician and was one of the many prominent mathematicians in the Bernoulli family.

title: Vitus Bering dies
subtitle: Danish-born Russian explorer
fact: Vitus Jonassen Bering, also known as Ivan Ivanovich Bering, was a Danish-born Russian cartographer, explorer, and officer in the Russian Navy.

title: Pierre-Augustin de Beaumarchais dies
subtitle: French playwright, diplomat and polymath
fact: Pierre-Augustin Caron de Beaumarchais was a French playwright and diplomat of the Age of Enlightenment.

title: Evangelista Torricelli dies
subtitle: Italian physicist and mathematician
fact: Evangelista Torricelli was an Italian physicist and mathematician, and a student of Benedetto Castelli.

title: Evliya Çelebi dies
subtitle: Turkish traveler and writer
fact: Dervish Mehmed Zillî, known as Evliya Çelebi, was an Ottoman Turkish explorer who travelled through his home country during its cultural zenith as well as neighboring lands.

title: François-Noël Babeuf dies
subtitle: French political agitator and journalist
fact: François-Noël Babeuf, also known as Gracchus Babeuf, was a French proto-communist, revolutionary, and journalist of the French Revolutionary period.

title: Jean-François de Galaup, comte de La Pérouse dies
subtitle: French Navy officer and explorer
fact: Chef d'escadre Jean François de Galaup, comte de Lapérouse was a French Navy officer and explorer.

title: Frančesco Redi dies
subtitle: Italian entomologist and poets
fact: Francesco Redi was an Italian physician, naturalist, biologist, and poet.

title: John Aubrey dies
subtitle: English writer and antiquarian
fact: John Aubrey was an English antiquary, natural philosopher and writer.

title: Jan Baptist van Helmont dies
subtitle: Chemist, physiologist, and physician from the Spanish Low Countries
fact: Jan Baptist van Helmont was a chemist, physiologist, and physician from Brussels.

title: Willem Barents dies
subtitle: Dutch navigator, cartographer, and Arctic explorer
fact: Willem Barentsz, anglicized as William Barents or Barentz, was a Dutch navigator, cartographer, and Arctic explorer.

title: Avvakum dies
subtitle: Russian protopope
fact: Avvakum Petrov was a Russian Old Believer and protopope of the Kazan Cathedral on Red Square who led the opposition to Patriarch Nikon's reforms of the Russian Orthodox Church.

title: Christian Goldbach dies
subtitle: German mathematician
fact: Christian Goldbach was a Prussian mathematician connected with some important research mainly in number theory; he also studied law and took an interest in and a role in the Russian court.

title: Rigas Velestinlis dies
subtitle: Greek philosopher
fact: Rigas Feraios or Velestinlis, born as Antonios Rigas Velestinlis, was a Greek writer, political thinker and revolutionary, active in the Modern Greek Enlightenment.

title: John Wilkins dies
subtitle: Secretary of the Royal Society and Bishop of Chester
fact: John Wilkins was an English Anglican clergyman, natural philosopher, and author, and was one of the founders of the Royal Society.

title: Georg Forster dies
subtitle: German naturalist, ethnologist, travel writer, journalist, and revolutionary
fact: Johann George Adam Forster, also known as Georg Forster, was a German-Polish geographer, naturalist, ethnologist, travel writer, journalist and revolutionary.

title: Pietro Della Valle dies
subtitle: Italian explorer and composer
fact: Pietro Della Valle, also written Pietro della Valle, was an Italian composer, musicologist, and author who travelled throughout Asia during the 17th century.

title: Christian Thomasius dies
subtitle: German philosopher
fact: Christian Thomasius was a German jurist and philosopher.
